title: Alphabet Q4 and Fiscal Year 2025 Earnings Highlights
text: Alphabet reported strong fourth-quarter 2025 results for the period ended December 31, 2025. Consolidated revenue increased 18% year over year to $113.8 billion, reflecting momentum in both Google Services and Google Cloud. Google Services revenue rose 14% to $95.9 billion, led by growth in Search, subscriptions, platforms, devices, and YouTube ads. Google Cloud revenue increased 48% to $17.7 billion, driven by demand for Google Cloud Platform, enterprise AI infrastructure, enterprise AI solutions, and core cloud products. Operating income increased 16%, and operating margin was 31.6%, despite a Waymo-related employee compensation charge. Net income increased 30%, and diluted EPS rose 31% to $2.82. For full-year 2025, Alphabet revenue exceeded $400 billion for the first time, reaching $402.8 billion, with net income of $132.2 billion. Management also highlighted strong Gemini usage, YouTube revenue above $60 billion across ads and subscriptions, and planned 2026 capital expenditures of $175 billion to $185 billion to support AI and infrastructure demand.
